hero.title: 12 Days, Char Dham.
hero.intro: The most revered pilgrimage in Hinduism. The Char Dham, the four sacred shrines of Yamunotri, Gangotri, Kedarnath and Badrinath, high in the Garhwal Himalayas. Twelve days of devotion, mountain roads and the source of the holy rivers.
hero.heroImageAlt: Char Dham Yatra Kedarnath Badrinath Himalayan shrines

overview.heading: 12 Days Char Dham Yatra: The Full Picture
body: The Char Dham Yatra is the holiest pilgrimage in Hinduism, visiting the four sacred shrines of the Garhwal Himalayas: Yamunotri, source of the Yamuna; Gangotri, source of the Ganges; Kedarnath, one of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Shiva; and Badrinath, sacred to Vishnu. Completing the circuit is the spiritual aspiration of a lifetime for many devotees.

This 12 day Char Dham package from Malaysia is arranged privately, beginning from Haridwar or Rishikesh and winding high into the mountains. The journey involves significant driving on mountain roads, and treks or pony rides to reach Yamunotri and Kedarnath. We provide yatra assistance throughout, with helicopter options available for Kedarnath. It is a demanding but deeply rewarding pilgrimage.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Kedarnath
items.body: One of the twelve Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva, set dramatically beneath snow peaks at over 3,500 metres, reached by a trek or pony ride (or helicopter). The most demanding and revered of the four shrines.

items.title: Badrinath
items.body: Sacred to Lord Vishnu, the colourful Badrinath temple sits in a stunning mountain setting near the Tibet border, with the Tapt Kund hot springs below.

items.title: Yamunotri & Gangotri
items.body: The sources of the holy Yamuna and Ganges rivers, high in the mountains, where the Char Dham pilgrimage begins amid glaciers, hot springs and dramatic scenery.

days.title: Arrive Haridwar / Rishikesh
days.description: Met at Dehradun airport and transferred to Haridwar for the evening Ganga Aarti.

days.title: To Barkot
days.description: Drive towards Yamunotri, overnight at Barkot.

days.title: Yamunotri
days.description: Trek or pony to Yamunotri, the first dham.
suggestedActivities: Trek or pony from Janki Chatti

days.title: Barkot to Uttarkashi
days.description: Drive towards Gangotri, overnight at Uttarkashi.

days.title: Gangotri
days.description: Visit Gangotri, the source shrine of the Ganges.

days.title: To Kedarnath
days.description: Trek, pony or helicopter to Kedarnath; overnight near the shrine.
suggestedActivities: Trek or pony from Gaurikund (or helicopter)

days.title: To Badrinath
days.description: Drive to Badrinath, the fourth dham, near the Tibet border.

days.title: Badrinath & Mana Village
days.description: Morning darshan and the last village before Tibet.

days.title: Badrinath to Rishikesh
days.description: Long scenic drive back down to Rishikesh.

days.title: Depart
days.description: Transfer to Dehradun or Delhi for your onward flight.

whyBook.heading: The Char Dham, Arranged With Care
whyBook.body: The Char Dham involves high altitude, mountain roads, treks and a narrow seasonal window. We handle the routing, the yatra assistance and helicopter options, with experienced mountain drivers. Dhesu has arranged Himalayan pilgrimages for many years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Char Dham Yatra from Malaysia
sections.body:
The Char Dham Yatra is the holiest pilgrimage in Hinduism, visiting the four sacred Himalayan shrines of Yamunotri, Gangotri, Kedarnath and Badrinath. This 12 day package handles the demanding logistics so you can focus on the devotion.

The journey involves significant mountain driving and treks or pony rides to reach Yamunotri and Kedarnath, with helicopter options available. We provide yatra assistance throughout and use experienced mountain drivers.

It is a once-in-a-lifetime pilgrimage for devotees, demanding but profoundly rewarding.

sections.heading: Season, Altitude and Preparation
sections.body:
The Char Dham shrines open roughly from late April or May to October or November, closing for the harsh Himalayan winter. This narrow window is the only time the yatra can be done, so booking well ahead is essential.

The high altitude and treks make a reasonable level of fitness important, and we strongly advise consulting your doctor before travelling. Helicopter and pony options reduce the physical demands of the most difficult sections, such as Kedarnath.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Dehradun or Delhi Airport (DED / DEL). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: When is the Char Dham Yatra open?
items.answer: The four shrines open roughly from late April or May to October or November, closing for the Himalayan winter. The exact dates vary each year with conditions. This narrow window is the only time the yatra can be undertaken, so book well in advance.

items.question: How physically demanding is the yatra?
items.answer: It is demanding. There is significant mountain driving, and reaching Yamunotri and Kedarnath involves treks or pony rides at altitude. Helicopter options are available for Kedarnath. A reasonable level of fitness is important, and we advise consulting your doctor before travelling.

items.question: Are helicopter options available?
items.answer: Yes. Helicopter services to Kedarnath, and helicopter-assisted Char Dham options, can reduce the physical demands and time involved. These are subject to availability and weather and carry additional cost. Let us know your preference when booking.
